title: Chemical Hazards
description: Questions about chemical substances, synthesis routes, and dispersal methods in your research.

questions.text: Does your research involve any chemicals on the following controlled lists?
questions.helpText: The CWC Schedules list chemicals relevant to chemical weapons (https://www.opcw.org/chemical-weapons-convention/annexes/annex-chemicals). The Australia Group list covers dual-use chemical precursors (https://www.dfat.gov.au/publications/minisite/theaustraliagroupnet/site/en/precursors.html). If unsure, check with your institution's chemical safety officer.

options.label: CWC Schedule 1 (chemicals with few or no uses outside weapons)
CWC Schedule 2 (significant risk to the Convention; some commercial use)
CWC Schedule 3 (previously produced as weapons; large-scale industrial use)
None of the above / not sure

signals.concernDescription: Your research involves a CWC Schedule 1 chemical — substances with few or no uses outside chemical weapons. This carries the highest level of chemical dual-use concern.
Your research involves a CWC Schedule 2 chemical, which poses significant risk under the Chemical Weapons Convention.
Your research involves a CWC Schedule 3 chemical. These have been used as weapons historically but also have widespread industrial applications.
Your research involves a chemical on the Australia Group precursors list, which carries export control implications.

questions.text: Does your synthesis route use or produce intermediates that appear on controlled lists, even if they are not your target product?

signals.concernDescription: Your synthesis route passes through controlled intermediates. Even though they are not your target product, their presence requires management.
You were unsure whether your synthesis route involves controlled intermediates. Consider reviewing your route against the CWC schedules and Australia Group lists.

questions.text: Could your synthesis methods or routes be adapted to produce chemical weapons agents or their precursors?

signals.concernTitle: Synthesis route adaptable to CW agent production
signals.concernDescription: Your synthesis methods could be adapted for producing chemical weapons agents or precursors. This is a significant dual-use concern.
You were unsure whether your methods could be adapted for harmful purposes. Consider seeking specialist assessment.

questions.text: Does your research involve novel compounds with significant toxicity, or methods for enhancing the toxicity or bioavailability of existing compounds?

signals.concernDescription: Your research involves creating or enhancing toxic compounds. The dual-use dimension depends on the specific toxicity profile and accessibility.
You were unsure about the significance of toxicity in your compounds.

questions.text: Does your work involve aerosolisation, encapsulation, or dispersal mechanisms that could be adapted for delivery of harmful agents?

signals.concernDescription: Your work on dispersal mechanisms could be adapted for delivery of harmful agents. This is a significant concern even when the intended application is beneficial.
You were unsure whether your dispersal methods could be adapted for harmful delivery.
